reviews.review_text: Stayed 3 nights. Cabin was neat and tidy as was the entire park. Staff were friendly and helpful. Great shower with heaps of water pressure.
Stayed few time on business matters so comfy great price staff great easy accessible  cabins had everything.    Will come again.
The Cabins and park are very clean. The Caravan park has a large country town feel with everything close including a regular train service to the City of Adelaide.

Nice clean park but beware of the red and white barriers on the left.  Very tight turn. Worst turn in on our trip so far.|Take a wide berth if turning left into park, don’t cut the corner.  Better still, drive up the road turn around and come back making a right turn into the park.
A small, quiet caravan park. Good, secure location and spacious sites with plenty of shade throughout. Walking distance to the train station and town centre.
Gawler is one of the most under rated places in South Australia. It has a wonderful range of hotels, restaurants and cafes. The Library is sensational and people are friendly. Well worth a visit.

Booked a cabin, about what you'd expect for the price. The problem is their people (staff/manager). I'm all good with unfriendly, people have bad days and I don't need nice. But this was the only place I've felt threatened. Not cool and not worth your time. Give this place a pass.

Great little caravan parks. Easy car parking, laundrett modern and easy to use and it's clean.. cabins are a little old but has everything you need including heating and cooler.  The only thing was there is a cooktop/ oven but there weren't any trays to put food on to cook  in the oven...

Very basic accomodation. Cabin quite small. Positives - clean. Negatives - poor tv reception / tv not tuned to very limited number tv stations / quite poor tv reception further limiting choices / very limited wifi of 1gb per cabin per day / no pots or pans in kitchen / main bedroom very small / very limited bathroom soaps provided / cabins whilst neat generally dated / very limited play equipment for kids / no utensils at public bbq area. Anyway suppose you get what you pay for but have stayed at other three star parks and have been better equipped.

Really wonderful villa clean fresh had everything we needed for a very comfortable stay on the weekend close enough to all the shops and restaurants and hotels |All within walking distance |Was a very pleasant and quiet location could not wish for anything else price was great can’t wait to go back at some point for another stay cheers John
Bloody Great
Very friendly  service

Very clean caravan park, quiet.
Bathroom facilities well maintained and clean.

Dogs barking from 6am. No matter how clean, how lovely the staff, pets ruin it.
Night time barking was bearable but early morning make it ridiculous after London travels.

We stayed here on a powered site while we were working and visiting family and highly recommend!The site was perfect and the amenities are immaculate!!. Perfect quiet place to head into town or the barossa to explore.
The owners Jenny and miles are so helpful and friendly and pepper is the perfect gentleman even when our little Nala is barking in his face!.
Lastly I cannot thank Julie and snow enough for being absolute legends and helping us out when we needed it. you guys are amazing people and we will definitely be back to stay again when we come back through. P.s Nala misses her visits with you too!😊

1 star given to the arrogant male manager and admin. Seriously!!….when you have driven from interstate (all day) last thing you want is to be greeted by the grumpiness and unhappy faces at reception like they were the one’s who had just done the long haul..just wow and disgusting attitude. We are very unimpressed with our first G’Day Park stay and it’s horrible service provided. We certainly would not recommend this park to anyone. Gawler caravan park Do Better towards your paying customers it cost nothing to be nice and friendly!!!

WORST PLACE IVE EVER STAYED.
Cheers to the male on-site manager for completely ruining my family's experience at this caravan park.
Not being locals and here for a funeral we were unaware of the parking situation due to lack of signs and were absolutely abused and humiliated by this man, who yelled at us from the road.

No Parking signs would be a great idea to keep that mongrel on a leash!

Not only that, our cabin's front door didnt close properly or lock and was wide open when we woke up in the morning.

The bed's were also extremely uncomfortable.

Word of advise...always read the reviews before booking. Terrible experience and the worst customer service ive ever had.
Great location. Utilised train into Adelaide city.  Easy distance out to Barossa Valley.  Very good amenities.

As an ex Gawler Girl travelling back to Gawler for a visit and staying at the caravan park first a couple of years back in a cabin (which was lovely and clean and very comfortable) and this time in a Motorhome on a powered site I have no complaints.  Easy access to our site, toilets and facilities close by and very clean, and was even able to use the laundry late at night to get our washing done before hitting the road early next morning.  Highly recommended 👌
Stayed in a cabin on a very hot weekend. Airconditioning worked perfectly.|The cabin was spotless and the staff was friendly

Reception staff were INCREDIBLY rude. Despite AMPLE space out the front of our villa, we were told we weren’t able to bring our trailer in. The only option given was to park it next the motorhome of said reception staff member. As it contained all of our travel belongings, as well as Christmas gifts, we weren’t comfortable with this. Luckily, we had family down the road and stored it there, but VERY inconvenient. The family villa was a generous size, with a nice outdoor decking space. The villa itself was tidy but floors and bathroom were dirty. The bathroom had an interesting set up, and didn’t have any air flow. We arrived on a warm summer day. No aircon had been put on prior, and it took until 9pm to cool the villa down enough to be comfortable. If we hadn’t have already paid, we would have stayed elsewhere.
Great little park close to everything.  Clean, great amenities and friendly staff.

I had a lovely stay here in a pet friendly cabin. The staff are helpful and the park is well maintained and cabins very clean. I liked the green spaces between each pet friendly cabin for my dog to play on. Close to Adelaide but you still get that county feel with the Gawler River running behind the park. Lots to do within walking distance. Ruby and I hope to return soon. Thank you for a restful stay.

It is on the main road, so patience is needed to turn right. Very clean amenities and well kept grounds. Julz and all the staff are helpful and friendly. There is wildlife around.
Walking distance to train and shops.

Central location, so comes with a bit of road noise, which dies down in the evening. Generous sites, spotless amenities, dog friendly. Great parklands nearby and shops in walking distance. Grounds kept very nicely. Gawler is a great place to base yourself, only half hour from the Barossa Valley and reasonably priced
Had a villa. The traffic noise is absolutely horrific. No sleep for 2 nights. Why would you place them against the busiest road in Gawler???
Never again
Villas next to busy road. Very noisy and impossible to sleep.

Caravan park is brilliant and the staff very helpful.
Amenities are very clean.
Lovely caravan park with very clean ammenities. Close to station and many attractions. Would definitely stay here again!

What a great caravan park. Toilets and showers incredibly clean.
And the staff are so friendly and helpful. Probably one of the nicest parks we've stayed in

Amazing service and helpfull staff highly recommended.
We stayed in our camper in a powered site .
Amenities were clean and tidy
Go stay and have a wonderfull time

Just spent 3 months in Gawler Caravan Park whilst attending to some medical needs. The reception staff were exceptional in meeting our needs. The facilities were all very well maintained and clean and the park is very close to the train, shops and Barossa Valley attractions. Thanks for making our difficult time very easy. 😊
Gawler Caravan Park is central to town; lovely parkland walks in close vicinity; sites are not too cramped. All facilities are excellent & Most important- super clean and neat.

Stayed in a 2 person chalet, wasn't a fan. Very noisy by the road side and the walls are paper thin. The weird bathroom cubicle thing also gets very damp inside and is hard to air out. No curtain for high up windows above entrance too and so in the morning the entire place is filled with light even if you have all the blinds down.

Also, I hate to say this, but the floor was filthy, vacuumed sure, but definitely not mopped in ages because we both noticed about half way through the night that our feet were practically black with grime from walking around in the place.

Would not recommend, especially considering how comparatively expensive it was for what you get.

Great caravan park, lots of powered sites, cabins. Very clean amenities and grounds kept clean as. Very friendly and helpful staff close to main street and walking distance to shops. Dog friendly as well. Highly recommend stopping in if passing through.
Very friendly staff and clean facilities. Great location with walking distance to local shops and Gawler central train station.
Absolutely disgusting 🫣 😤 will NEVER book there again

Stayed in a studio villa and while it had some good points, it is not somewhere I can recommend.
The Good - location, parking, friendly staff, not crowded, decent sized villa.
The Bad - constant road noise, dirty floor, cheap mattress (could feel springs), stingy on toiletries, only 2 of everything so had to be constantly washing dishes/ cutlery, only 1 chair inside despite our friend's villa having 2, no curtains on high windows (wake up early to enjoy the heavy traffic), outdoor setting fraying, general low quality maintenance repairs (tap, shower screen).
The road noise was the worst I've experienced. There is a general air of cheapness and 'this will do', at least with the villa we were in. I don't mind places being a little old or worn out, but advertise accordingly. The villas at least are best described as slightly fancy dongas - good for a night or two, no more.
Nice spot easy to get to close to town centre recommend to all
Absolutely awesome place, great sites available for the caravan,  very neat and clean amenities, reception and staff were so helpful and friendly 😀.

Rented a cheaper cabins as the more expensive better appointed cabins were in use.
So basic Cabins with a fair bit of use wear, screen door and main cabin door were a problem to shut in particular and stairs to steep for old guys with back and knee problems tp climb.
But guess you get what you pay for, which incidentally was $130 per night.
Never been there
Clean room & good location

Stayed here 9 months ago in a lovely cabin, clean good location so returned for a 2 night stay. Sadly disappointed, opened the door to be greeted by the stench of sewerage. Mould on bathroom ceiling and floor. A totally different experience to last time
We had a camper. Good facilities. Just an overnight stay

Great park amenities very clean close to town walking distance the only thing was our site was small and on the corner couldn't put awning out because our car was parked right at our door friendly staff.

This is a beautiful place to stay, the owners were caretaking while managers on leave and they were absolutely lovely people. Bent over backwards to accommodate us and our two dogs. Everything is clean and tidy and they have everything you can think of. Highly recommend staying here, if just for a night or a bit longer you can’t go wrong. Plus it’s within walking distance to Gawlers Main Street  and shops and beautiful parks.
Cabins right next to main road you need ear plugs to go to sleep

This is a very nice park, not too large…we thought just right…The reception are so super friendly & helpful.|The park has nice parking bays for the caravan as well…..facilities are super clean..Town is walking distance & is a great old fashioned high street…I went walking through the park areas with the two little ones (the dogs) 7 I. Must say the park areas are beautiful…|This park will n out disappoint….Thank you so much..
Nice spot with easy access to shops

We stayed in one of the cabins that is towards the back of the establishment. It gets the job done but lacks cleanliness and has seen better days with clear signs of wear and tear. The bed was uncomfortable, and it was noisy having so many others so close. On the plus side, the laundry area was quiet well maintained and clean.
Nice park good size parking we had the area along the sea edge
The caravan lots at the bottom of the hill are nice and quiet. Beware of the traffic noise in the "deluxe " cabins at the topof the hill,  next to main north road
In a caravan , very clean bathrooms ,toilets  lovely people kind and honest thankyou

Great location to get around. Stayed back in March 2025. Amenities are really good. Great water pressure. Quiet and grounds are well taken care of. Slab sites and gravel sites. The park is a good size for large and small Caravans/camper trailers. Easy to book on line.
Great location. Train to Adelaide 5 mins walk next to a Coles. 15min into wine tasting in the Barrosa valley. Wake up to bird song.
Outstanding caravan park ..friendly staff and very helpful.. amenities very clean always..sites spacious.great location a must place to park your van etc for awhile ..central to all shops etc
Super friendly, helpful staff.  Cabin was clean, spacious and met all of my needs.  Thank you.
It's a nice friendly park

After hearing mixed reviews we were pleasantly surprised with this caravan park. We stayed 5 nights, may 2025, ammenity block was immaculate, friendly accommodating staff..told them how big our caravan was they gave us a good site to accommodate our caravan.

Sorry some others didn't have the same experience we had.
It's a smallish park, very tidy, just wish people would pick up their dog poop.
We were on site 59..good size sites backing onto the river.

We will be back for sure

Absolutely blown away with the help we received from receptionist, so friendly and caring as we were due to leave and a big storm front hit the state, she sorted us an extra night and didn’t have to move sites. Amenities were bloody spotless and well maintained. Location close to town also made it an ideal location.

Pulled in without a booking, fitted us is, tight squeeze but ok.
Hubby had a shower, loved the hot water and shower pressure,stated was clean. I went on, yes amenities are super clean, laundry is great.
Small park,very tidy.
BUT very dissapointed at the $46 charge, one night as there was not a working BBQ to cook dinner. This place is missing a camp kitchen would be more suitable if there was one, especially when pulling in late and need to cook after travelling. Maybe we have been lucky and got use to camp kitchens ?
Excellent in every way 👌😁....within walking distance to shops & station.

Booked power site. Staff at reception weren't really engaging when checking in although they weren't horrible either. Site was bloody terrible...never in my whole camping experience have I camped where you couldn't hammer a peg into the ground not even partially. No grass just scoria and hard as granite. Not recommended.
Good park, cabins are clean and well maintained.
1st class all the way - beautifully clean and tidy throughout the whole park.  We will be back for sure

My wife and I enjoyed our stay here,the staff were very friendly 😊 the weekend that we stayed was extremely hot and we thought a split system air-conditioning would have been a lot better than the old wall air-conditioning. The caravan location was outstanding for seeing the local area and the shopping centre
Great place to stay.  Cabins are neat and clean and surprisingly quiet.
For us Grey Nomads, caravan park is ideal. Love the spot.

Unfortunately couldn’t give good rating. Had to check out in 2 hours after check in. Room AirCon was crap so couldn’t stay there. Receptionist gave a pedestal fan which couldn’t make the room comfortable at all. Toilets 🚽 and shower need to be fixed and upgraded.

Booked a drive through site for two nights.
Told them my rig was 14.7m
Site nowhere near able to park there
Arrived 9pm after a 13 hour drive with area I was in packed with cars > 2 on some sites,
Got down the lane with inches to spare
Rang office to say could not fit
Checked out this morning and they refused to refund the second night ,
Had not connected to power or water
Very very misleading advertising to say they have " "drive through sites "
Do not stay at this park,

We checked in today and after staying 3 hours we had to leave. We booked for two nights, however today and tomorrow the temperatures are 36 and 38 respectively. When we entered the room, thankfully the aircon was on but after waiting for 1 hour it didn’t cool down the rooms. The aircon is positioned in the lounge / dining and no aircon in the two bedrooms. On a nice day the small aircon could be sufficient but not on a day when the temperature is more than 35. We called the after hours mobile number and asked if they have any other villa/ cabins available, she said no. Then we asked for at least a fan and she was nice enough to give us the feeble office fan. We waited for the 2 nd bedroom to cool down a bit with the aircon and the fan in the middle room. However it didn’t work, which was not a surprise. The shower rail in the bathroom is positioned in a way that if someone is unmindful they can bump their head when getting up from the toilet. It happened with both my child and husband!
We did ask the lady if the charge for at least one night can be refunded as they are unable to either upgrade our room or provide a portable aircon for the second room. She said since it is “change of mind” nothing can be refunded although we stayed for 3 hours instead of two nights!
Powered on-site.   Lots of space,  clean tidy amenities. Staff are good people to deal with.  Highly recommend, close to trains and main street (walking distance if your up for a stroll )
A lot of shady spots, except the middle of the park. They are in full sun. Very clean amenities.
Great park with spotless amenities, easy access and lovely friendly owners. Have stayed before and will definitely stay again. Highly Recommended AAAA++++
Clean and well appointed laundry facilities .

Fantastic staff , good back in sites
Very quiet and safe
Easy late check in. The cabin was clean and tidy with good kitchen facilities. Lots of road noise. Poor Telstra phone service in the bedroom, and wifi doesn’t reach the cabin well.
It had everything we needed. Even loved having a dog park just down the road. The only tragedy, is that it is in Gawler where there is no where to park with a caravan - not even at Bunnings.

Great place to stay for a few days and check out Gawler
Do yourself a favour check it out cheers Peter 🍻

I recently spent 10 nights at the Gawler Caravan Park, and my experience was just okay. The park's location is its strongest aspect - it's within easy walking distance to Gawler's center, shops, pubs, and restaurants. Public transport is also convenient, with the Gawler Central train station nearby.

However, the powered sites are poorly laid out, and the amenities are not centrally located, making for a long and sometimes an uncomfortable walk to the bathroom, especially at night. The camp kitchen is also very basic and underwhelming with only the most basic cooking facilities

My main gripe is the price - over $40 per night for a powered site feels excessive, especially considering the park seems to prioritize cabin customers over caravan patrons.

Overall, the park is average, and while its location is a plus, the drawbacks and high price might make me consider alternative options in the future.
Great customer service, cabins were super clean. Staff friendly. Location great. I recommend Gawler caravan park and pet friendly which is awesome.

Lady at reception very unhappy disposition not welcoming/rude on interaction with her on a few occasions.
New cabins walls so thin can hear traffic all night!
Bathroom flooding and backing up!
Heating not effective as aircon undersized!
Won’t be staying here again!
Easy to access. Good layout and lovely friendly owners. We will come back.
Welcoming staff nice clean serviced accomadation

Absolutely recommended this caravan park.
They are so clean, friendly and it felt very safe.
Can't say enough about  the staff, they couldn't do enough to help your stay a good one.
STAFF VERY FRIENDLY AND HELPFUL
Great park no dramas at all arriving late
Nice place to stay

We have been at Gawler Caravan Park for 6 weeks of a 3 month stay, Jenny and Miles the Owners are lovely folks and provided ANZAC Breakfast on ANZAC Day. We are a 13 meter van and our site is plenty big enough which is great. The park is dog friendly and most owners pick up after their dogs and the dogs are well behaved. There is always the owners who cannot control their dogs and can’t stop them barking which is a reflection of them as owners and not the park. The Office Staff and Managers are very approachable and friendly, the facilities are always clean and well maintained. Great place to stay.

Snow and Julie are great managers have a lot of time for them.
The temporary managers are sarcastic, rude, no respect for tenants property (watering lawn area and wetting cars and Annex), and blame people with no evidence that they have done what they are blamed for.
My suggestion is get rid of them and get new temporary managers while Snow and Julie are on their week off.
Great.
Nothing flash but the bed was comfy.
Have my own van
Such rude staff. They are so rude ,if i was the owner they would have been sacked on the spot. Never again.

Amazing service by Julie catering to our every need. Great sites, services and clean amenities
Many thanks
Cabin was clean and comfy Close to everything but we found the noise from the very busy road quite unpleasant
Nice park and nice staff, facilities are clean and tidy
I had roaches climbing the roof and dropping on me. I was abused by 2 people. And refused a refund

Great location for a stop to explore the Barossa Valley region without paying the premium prices in the Valley region. It's very close to the railway line and Coles and is a very quiet and secure park. Has a river run right behind, but, unfortunately, it was bone dry when we were there in March. The amenities were spotless, and the park is dog friendly. The staff were very friendly as well. Would definitely recommend a stop over there.
Very friendly and helpful staff. Cabins clean. Enjoyed the stay!!
Good place to stay

Comfortable but fairly basic. A good option in the north of Adelaide. We stayed there while working in the area and felt safe during our stay. There are plenty of options for eating in the area with a number of pubs including the Golden Fleece.

I previously visited this park and thought it was lovely until I met a worker there and found him to be quite rude and obnoxious towards others at the park. Cabin sizes were lovely tho. Facilities were good also. But if I was the parks owners I would look at who you have working there, as it can be a turn off to other people visiting.
For a place to put your head down over night it does the job. Are pet friendly and close to shops
Nice and quiet and clean.

The newer villas are lovely....and were very clean,  which is very important to me.  The only downside is the location by an extremely busy main highway road,  with road noise constant.   Maybe a solid cement or brick fence instead of a tin one could camouflage the noise somewhat.  🤔

A very nice park close to everything you need.
Well presented and lovely and clean.
Thank you.
Credit to the staff.

Wait until you encounter the male manager at this park, you will leave very disappointed. Being yelled at like a dog is what you can expect, the owners of this park would do well to send him packing. He is extremely rude and way out of his depth when it comes to dealing with paying customers.

As for the park it is evident it is being run by incompetent management, the layout is very poorly set up leaving no room in some situations to park your vehicle safely. This is one Park I will never revisit.

This caravan park is great and spacious, good access to park the van and ease of driving around especially if you have a big van.
Staff are friendly and helpful.
Facilities are neat and tidy as well as secure.
The bbq area is very neat and user friendly with everything you need (even a well positioned t.v. for when your cooking).
Short walk into the main streets of Gawler with all that you need from coles, pubs, clothes etc.
Great park. Tidy, well managed.
Great service and affordable rates. Probably the cleanest amenities we have experienced in over 20 years of caravanning. The park is well laid out and very clean.
Best caravan park in Gawler . Snowy is a amazeballs . Come stay it’s like home
Staff need to lighten up
Cabin size is great. Could sleep 4peope in relative comfort. The kitchen layout makes it hard to prepare food for 4 or more people. Visitors have only 3 dedicated parking spaces outside the gates.

When I rang to book our stay I was very clear with the lady on the phone on what we needed; I gave her the dimension of our camper trailer, told her it was a soft base that needed to be pegged in and opened to the left of the draw bar. She told me the site number that should work and that was the last I heard.

When we arrived we were met with exemplary customer service but I was then presented with the map showing me to a different site, at this point I thought nothing of it as I assumed that maybe she had worked out a better option for us. However, when we arrived at the site we quickly noticed that this wasn't the case and the site was a large concrete slab with the gravel spot, which was needed to peg our camper tent down, out to the right. We did a quick lap and it was obvious that the place had no other options for us, plus we had just been in the car for 3hrs with 3 kids and had plans we were running late for so we wanted to get set up quickly, so we made do by stretching ropes across the concrete to secure the end of the tent but it was definitely not ideal. The site also backed onto the creek that I specifically asked to be away from as I had a toddler; although, this turned out to be not so bad and having a direct line of site to the playground was convenient as the kids could run down there and play while we were setting up the camper and yet we could still easily keep an eye on them too.

The really frustrating part is that I took a look at the site I was told about over the phone and it was definitely a better option so obviously someone moved us for someone elses convenience and ignored the requirements I expressed at booking.

At the end of it all it was really just a place to sleep for the weekend while we were attending family events locally and as much as it wasn't a horrible experience we will not be returning to this park as we feel it just isn't suitable for our needs anyway but I just wish someone had recognised that the site we were given was not suitable for the information I had made the effort to provide them.

Despite the 42 degree heat the air-conditioning was on when we arrived. Made welcome was close to amenities everything Cosi said there was. Bit noisie due to traffic but got use to it after a while. In all dam good value for money.
Very nice park, great facilities and close enough to centre to walk in. Couple of nice walks you can do from the park too. Would recommend 😁.
Great Park friendly staff, clean amenities will come back
Love that they have pet friendly cabins at a very reasonable price.
Nice clean park close to town Nice amenities
Great park, close to wine regions and Adelaide. Excellent helpful park management 👏.

Very clean and compact park. Variety of cabins. Best laundry I've seen. Toilets and showers A1. Grassed area to walk your pet. Camp kitchen and BBQs clean and well equipped. My only gripe, I asked for a grass site and was placed over blue metal.
Hot plate (not working). Stove exhaust (not working). Air-conditioning (not working). Bathroom exhaust (not working). Curtains fell upon closing due to broken curtain rail bracket.

Wonderful park with lovely staff. Very welcoming and friendly. I have stayed at this park on multiple occasions over the years and can't fault the park. The staff recently allowed to use their accessible facilities regularly for showers following a leg injury with no worries which was very helpful. Would absolutely recommend staying here.

Small cabin but everything worked, this cabin had a special key to turn on the power, which needed to be removed when you locked the door to go out. Meaning nothing could be left charging while out. But the AC heated the room up fast so no big deal.

UPDATE 2023 - over priced, I know things have gone up but it was almost $200 a night, much better places along the road have been half the cost for better.
Its ok but not the best for kids and also has minimal amenities, great for self sufficient caravans.

Clean and friendly tourist Park. Boom gate entry has been updated to use a code instead of keys. Very hospitable managers. Pet friendly cabins and villas.
Our return visit after a year has not been disappointing. Park is well maintained and all staff are friendly and helpful. Our dog is very relaxed staying here.
Great place to hang, close to all shops, pubs etc
Well done G C P I'll be back.
Still here and loving it
Absolutely great place and friendly staff I recommend it for those that are travelling or just needing a place to stay
Clean tidy nd friendly

Stunning Location
Buitiful nature
Old trees
Clean rooms
Room for tent
Good service

Beautiful Caravan Park good size gravel van sites, some with concrete pads. Amenities are modern and well maintained. Gawler is a large town and a few minutes drive from the Park Definitely worth a stay .
Did visit at this stage. We plan to in the future. Friends say it's a good place to spend a few days.
Just brilliant! Perfect location, cute-as, clean, well-appointed 1br villa. 10/10 would stay again!!

Beautiful spot and great park. The owners are very welcoming and friendly. The park is clean and tidy as are the amenities.
Beautiful walking near the park along the creek. Not far from the town centre.
Great location but watch our for busy traffic times. Great grass areas for pets.
Lovely staff.  Large sites.  Very convenient access to Gawler.
